title: Terrorist attacks, deaths and injuries
subtitle: Confirmed deaths and injuries include all victims and attackers who died or were hurt because of a [terrorist attack](#dod:terrorism).
caption:
Terrorist attacks, deaths and injuries

Confirmed deaths and injuries include all victims and attackers who died or were hurt because of a terrorist attack.

This line chart tracks global trends in terrorist attacks, deaths, and non-fatal injuries from 1970 to 2021. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 50,000, while the horizontal axis marks years in ten-year increments. Three distinct lines represent the data: a blue line for the number of attacks, a brown line for deaths, and a red line for non-fatal injuries. From 1970 through the late 1990s, all three metrics remain relatively low, generally staying below 10,000, with a notable spike in non-fatal injuries around 2001 that reaches nearly 30,000. A significant upward trend begins around 2010 for all categories. The number of attacks, represented by the blue line, rises to a peak of approximately 17,000 around 2014 before declining to under 10,000 by 2021. The lines for deaths and non-fatal injuries show a much steeper increase during this period, both surging past the 40,000 mark between 2014 and 2015. Following this peak, both casualty metrics drop sharply, falling to roughly 20,000 for deaths and 15,000 for non-fatal injuries by the end of the timeline. Throughout the period after 2010, the values for deaths and non-fatal injuries track closely together, often crossing, while the count of attacks remains consistently lower than the casualty figures.
